message.content:
You are confirmer #2, independent. Confirm a production-hardening fix is BOTH appropriate (correct, no regression, idiomatic) AND complete (fully closes it; for a code change the named regression test must DISCRIMINATE — fail if the fix were reverted). Repo: /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay
Fix G-015 in .github/workflows/ci.yml
What: SBOM step applies the same .pip-audit-ignore allowlist so an accepted advisory doesn't fail SBOM generation.
Test(s): test_sbom_step_applies_the_allowlist
Steps: read the actual changed file(s); read the test and judge whether it discriminates; check no regression. For a doc/refactor item, judge correctness/coverage rather than revert-discrimination. Verdict CONFIRMED (both true) or CONCERN (something missing/wrong/under-tested). Be skeptical and specific.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.text: I'll verify the G-015 fix. Let me start by examining the changed files and test.

input.command: cd /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay && git log --oneline -5 && echo "---" && git diff HEAD~3 -- .github/workflows/ci.yml | head -120
input.description: Show recent commits and CI workflow diff

content.content:
7dccfd8 fix(hardening-p4): batch E — egress, error contracts, PII & test discrimination (8 findings)
c49261e fix(hardening-p4): batch D — CI / supply-chain reproducibility (G-015,016,022)
2eea958 fix(hardening-p4): batch C — provenance, observability & resample memory (G-009,010,012,023)
91dfb1a fix(hardening-p4): batch B — concurrency, reasoning-tier & serialization (G-002,003,004)
d61bd4e fix(hardening-p4): batch A — methodology integrity (G-001,005,006,011,017)
---
diff --git a/.github/workflows/ci.yml b/.github/workflows/ci.yml
index 91309f3..7b04a94 100644
--- a/.github/workflows/ci.yml
+++ b/.github/workflows/ci.yml
@@ -94,7 +94,11 @@ jobs:
         with:
           python-version: "3.12"
       - name: Install pip-audit
-        run: python -m pip install --upgrade pip pip-audit
+        # Pin the audit tooling to exact versions (#G-022): an unpinned pip-audit can change
+        # advisory results / exit behavior between releases, making the supply-chain gate
+        # non-reproducible (a green commit could fail tomorrow on a tooling bump) — the analogue
+        # of the uv pin (#F-031).
+        run: python -m pip install --upgrade pip && python -m pip install pip-audit==2.10.1
       - name: Lockfiles are in sync with pyproject (reproducibility guard)
         run: |
           # Pin uv to the exact version the lockfiles were generated with (#F-031): an unpinned
@@ -132,7 +136,19 @@ jobs:
           # downstream license/transitive monitoring has a stable artifact to track (#147). No
           # `|| true` mask (#F-012): a pip-audit failure must fail the step, and the SBOM must be
           # non-empty, so a broken/absent SBOM cannot pass CI green.
-          pip-audit --progress-spinner off -r requirements.lock --format cyclonedx-json -o sbom.cyclonedx.json
+          # Apply the SAME allowlist as the gate (#G-015): pip-audit exits non-zero on ANY finding
+          # regardless of output format, so without the allowlist an ACCEPTED advisory would fail
+          # SBOM generation here even though the gate step passed — coupling SBOM emission to vuln
+          # status and making the allowlist ineffective. The gate step already ran first, so a
+          # NON-allowlisted advisory has failed the job before we reach here.
+          IGNORES=()
+          if [ -f .pip-audit-ignore ]; then
+            while IFS= read -r id || [ -n "$id" ]; do
+              case "$id" in ''|\#*) continue ;; esac
+              IGNORES+=(--ignore-vuln "$id")
+            done < .pip-audit-ignore
+          fi
+          pip-audit --progress-spinner off -r requirements.lock --format cyclonedx-json -o sbom.cyclonedx.json "${IGNORES[@]}"
           test -s sbom.cyclonedx.json
           echo "SBOM emitted ($(wc -c < sbom.cyclonedx.json) bytes)"
       - name: Upload SBOM artifact
@@ -151,7 +167,7 @@ jobs:
           # lockfile pins (#F-013). Advisory monitoring of the rest is handled by Dependabot.
           pip install --require-hashes -r requirements.lock
           pip install --no-deps -e .
-          python -m pip install pip-licenses
+          python -m pip install pip-licenses==5.5.5  # pinned for reproducible license output (#G-022)
           pip-licenses --format=json --with-system > licenses.json
           python scripts/license_gate.py licenses.json
       - name: Upload license report

content.text: Let me look at the gate step (to compare allowlist handling) and find the test.

content.content:
90	    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
91	    steps:
92	      - uses: actions/checkout@34e114876b0b11c390a56381ad16ebd13914f8d5 # v4
93	      - uses: actions/setup-python@a26af69be951a213d495a4c3e4e4022e16d87065 # v5
94	        with:
95	          python-version: "3.12"
96	      - name: Install pip-audit
97	        # Pin the audit tooling to exact versions (#G-022): an unpinned pip-audit can change
98	        # advisory results / exit behavior between releases, making the supply-chain gate
99	        # non-reproducible (a green commit could fail tomorrow on a tooling bump) — the analogue
100	        # of the uv pin (#F-031).
101	        run: python -m pip install --upgrade pip && python -m pip install pip-audit==2.10.1
102	      - name: Lockfiles are in sync with pyproject (reproducibility guard)
103	        run: |
104	          # Pin uv to the exact version the lockfiles were generated with (#F-031): an unpinned
105	          # `uv` upgrade can change output format/ordering/annotations and produce spurious diffs
106	          # that fail this gate on every PR even when the lockfiles are current.
107	          python -m pip install uv==0.11.21
108	          uv pip compile pyproject.toml --all-extras --universal --generate-hashes -o /tmp/requirements.regen.lock
109	          # ignore the auto-generated header (timestamp/command line) when diffing
110	          diff <(grep -v '^#' requirements.lock) <(grep -v '^#' /tmp/requirements.regen.lock) \
111	            || { echo "::error::requirements.lock is stale — run: uv pip compile pyproject.toml --all-extras --universal --generate-hashes -o requirements.lock"; exit 1; }
112	          # the core lockfile (dev extra only) backs the hash-pinned core lane (#F-004) and must
113	          # stay in sync too.
114	          uv pip compile pyproject.toml --extra dev --universal --generate-hashes -o /tmp/requirements-core.regen.lock
115	          diff <(grep -v '^#' requirements-core.lock) <(grep -v '^#' /tmp/requirements-core.regen.lock) \
116	            || { echo "::error::requirements-core.lock is stale — run: uv pip compile pyproject.toml --extra dev --universal --generate-hashes -o requirements-core.lock"; exit 1; }
117	      - name: pip-audit (reproducible known-vulnerability scan)
118	        run: |
119	          set -euo pipefail
120	          # --strict: any dependency that cannot be collected/audited FAILS the gate (no silent
121	          # skips). --require-hashes: every pin must carry a hash. Build --ignore-vuln args as an
122	          # array so allowlist entries can't word-split or glob-expand, and read the file
123	          # newline-agnostically so the last line is honored even without a trailing newline.
124	          IGNORES=()
125	          if [ -f .pip-audit-ignore ]; then
126	            while IFS= read -r id || [ -n "$id" ]; do
127	              case "$id" in ''|\#*) continue ;; esac
128	              IGNORES+=(--ignore-vuln "$id")
129	            done < .pip-audit-ignore
130	          fi
131	          pip-audit --strict --require-hashes --progress-spinner off -r requirements.lock "${IGNORES[@]}"
132	      - name: SBOM (CycloneDX) from the lockfile
133	        run: |
134	          set -euo pipefail
135	          # Emit a machine-readable software bill of materials for the pinned dependency set so
136	          # downstream license/transitive monitoring has a stable artifact to track (#147). No
137	          # `|| true` mask (#F-012): a pip-audit failure must fail the step, and the SBOM must be
138	          # non-empty, so a broken/absent SBOM cannot pass CI green.
139	          # Apply the SAME allowlist as the gate (#G-015): pip-audit exits non-zero on ANY finding
140	          # regardless of output format, so without the allowlist an ACCEPTED advisory would fail
141	          # SBOM generation here even though the gate step passed — coupling SBOM emission to vuln
142	          # status and making the allowlist ineffective. The gate step already ran first, so a
143	          # NON-allowlisted advisory has failed the job before we reach here.
144	          IGNORES=()
145	          if [ -f .pip-audit-ignore ]; then
146	            while IFS= read -r id || [ -n "$id" ]; do
147	              case "$id" in ''|\#*) continue ;; esac
148	              IGNORES+=(--ignore-vuln "$id")
149	            done < .pip-audit-ignore
150	          fi
151	          pip-audit --progress-spinner off -r requirements.lock --format cyclonedx-json -o sbom.cyclonedx.json "${IGNORES[@]}"
152	          test -s sbom.cyclonedx.json
153	          echo "SBOM emitted ($(wc -c < sbom.cyclonedx.json) bytes)"
154	      - name: Upload SBOM artifact
155	        if: always()
156	        uses: actions/upload-artifact@ea165f8d65b6e75b540449e92b4886f43607fa02 # v4
157	        with:
158	          name: sbom-cyclonedx
159	          path: sbom.cyclonedx.json
160	          if-no-files-found: error
161	      - name: License gate (deny strong-copyleft in the pinned dependency set)
162	        run: |
163	          set -euo pipefail
164	          # Fail if any dependency carries a strong-copyleft license incompatible with the
165	          # Apache-2.0 distribution (#147). Scan the SAME hash-pinned set pip-audit audited, not a
166	          # live `pip install -e .[all]` resolution that could pull different versions than the
167	          # lockfile pins (#F-013). Advisory monitoring of the rest is handled by Dependabot.
168	          pip install --require-hashes -r requirements.lock
169	          pip install --no-deps -e .
170	          python -m pip install pip-licenses==5.5.5  # pinned for reproducible license output (#G-022)
171	          pip-licenses --format=json --with-system > licenses.json
172	          python scripts/license_gate.py licenses.json
173	      - name: Upload license report
174	        if: always()
175	        uses: actions/upload-artifact@ea165f8d65b6e75b540449e92b4886f43607fa02 # v4
176	        with:
177	          name: license-report
178	          path: licenses.json
179	          if-no-files-found: warn

def test_audit_tooling_is_version_pinned():
    # #G-022: pip-audit and pip-licenses must be pinned so the supply-chain gate is reproducible
    # run-to-run (a tooling bump can't silently change advisory/license results).
    text = _ci_text()
    assert "pip-audit==" in text, "pip-audit not version-pinned (#G-022)"
    assert "pip-licenses==" in text, "pip-licenses not version-pinned (#G-022)"
    assert "install --upgrade pip pip-audit\n" not in text, "unpinned pip-audit install reintroduced"


def test_dependabot_lockfile_regen_is_documented():
    # #G-016: Dependabot can't regenerate the uv lockfiles, so the manual regen path must be
    # documented + scripted, or every Dependabot PR is stuck on the stale-lock gate.
    regen = _ROOT / "scripts" / "regenerate_lockfiles.sh"
    assert regen.exists(), "no scripts/regenerate_lockfiles.sh (#G-016)"
    body = regen.read_text(encoding="utf-8")
    assert "uv pip compile" in body and "requirements-core.lock" in body
    db = (_ROOT / ".github" / "dependabot.yml").read_text(encoding="utf-8")
    assert "regenerate_lockfiles.sh" in db, "dependabot.yml does not point at the regen script"

Now verify discrimination: the test asserts the exact string `cyclonedx-json -o sbom.cyclonedx.json "${IGNORES[@]}"` plus `--ignore-vuln` and `.pip-audit-ignore` within the SBOM step slice. content.text:
Clean restore, working tree matches committed state. 

Let me assess correctness of the SBOM allowlist logic itself. The SBOM step duplicates the gate's allowlist-building block verbatim (array-based `--ignore-vuln`, newline-agnostic read, comment/blank skip), appends `"${IGNORES[@]}"` to the pip-audit invocation. This is correct and idiomatic—identical to the already-hardened gate step. The reasoning in the comment is sound: pip-audit exits non-zero on any finding regardless of `--format`, so without the allowlist an accepted advisory would fail SBOM generation under `set -euo pipefail`. The gate runs first, so non-allowlisted advisories already fail the job.

One minor note: with `set -euo pipefail` and `nounset` (`-u`), an empty array expansion `"${IGNORES[@]}"` is safe in bash 4.4+ (Ubuntu runner ships bash 5.x), and the gate step uses the identical pattern already in production—no regression. No `if-no-files-found`/SBOM emptiness behavior changed.

The fix is appropriate (correct, no regression, mirrors the gate exactly) and complete (the named test discriminates—fails on revert, passes on fix).

input.reasoning:
Fix in /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay/.github/workflows/ci.yml (commit c49261e): the "SBOM (CycloneDX) from the lockfile" step now builds the same `--ignore-vuln` allowlist from `.pip-audit-ignore` as the gate step and appends `"${IGNORES[@]}"` to the pip-audit invocation. Correctness: it duplicates the already-hardened gate block verbatim (array form prevents word-split/glob, newline-agnostic read honors a trailing-newline-less last line, blank/comment lines skipped). The rationale holds — pip-audit exits non-zero on any finding regardless of --format, so under `set -euo pipefail` an accepted advisory would otherwise fail SBOM generation even though the gate (which runs earlier) passed. No regression: empty-array expansion is safe on the Ubuntu bash 5.x runner, identical to the production gate step; SBOM emptiness check and upload behavior unchanged.

Test discrimination verified empirically: test_sbom_step_applies_the_allowlist (tests/test_supply_chain.py:243) slices the SBOM step text and asserts `--ignore-vuln`, `.pip-audit-ignore`, and the exact `cyclonedx-json -o sbom.cyclonedx.json "${IGNORES[@]}"` invocation are present. It PASSES on the current file and FAILS ("SBOM step does not apply the allowlist (#G-015)") when I reverted ci.yml to HEAD~3 — so it fails if the fix is reverted. Working tree restored clean afterward.
